PARAM.description: the character to recognize as a separator
PARAM.name: separator
PARAM.content: the character to recognize as a separator

RETURN.description: a splitter that uses the given single-character separator.
RETURN.content: a splitter that uses the given single-character separator.

PARAM.description: a CharMatcher that determines whether a character is a separator
PARAM.name: separatorMatcher
PARAM.content: a CharMatcher that determines whether a character is a separator

RETURN.description: a splitter that considers any single character matched by the given CharMatcher to be a separator.
RETURN.content: a splitter, with default settings, that uses this matcher

PARAM.description: the literal, nonempty string to recognize as a separator
PARAM.name: separator
PARAM.content: the literal, nonempty string to recognize as a separator

RETURN.description: a splitter that uses the given fixed string as a separator.
RETURN.content: a splitter, with default settings, that recognizes that separator

PARAM.description: the pattern that determines whether a subsequence is a separator. This pattern may not match the empty string.
PARAM.name: separatorPattern
PARAM.content: the pattern that determines whether a subsequence is a separator. This pattern may not match the empty string.

RETURN.description: a splitter that considers any subsequence matching pattern to be a separator.
RETURN.content: a splitter that considers any subsequence matching pattern to be a separator.

THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if {@code separatorPattern} matches the empty string
THROWS.description: if {@code separatorPattern} matches the empty string

PARAM.description: the pattern that determines whether a subsequence is a separator. This pattern may not match the empty string.
PARAM.name: separatorPattern
PARAM.content: the pattern that determines whether a subsequence is a separator. This pattern may not match the empty string.

RETURN.description: a splitter that considers any subsequence matching a given pattern (regular expression) to be a separator.
RETURN.content: a splitter, with default settings, that uses this pattern

THROWS.description: if separatorPattern is a malformed expression

THROWS.content: if separatorPattern is a malformed expression

THROWS.description: if separatorPattern matches the empty string
THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if separatorPattern matches the empty string

RETURN.content: a splitter that can split into fixed sized pieces

THROWS.description: if @code length is zero or negative
THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if @code length is zero or negative

RETURN.description: a splitter with the desired configuration.

PARAM.description: the maximum number of items returned

RETURN.description: a splitter with the desired configuration
RETURN.content: a splitter with the desired configuration

PARAM.description: a CharMatcher that determines whether a character should be removed from the beginning/end of a subsequence
PARAM.name: trimmer
PARAM.content: a CharMatcher that determines whether a character should be removed from the beginning/end of a subsequence

RETURN.content: Returns a splitter that behaves equivalently to this splitter, but removes all leading or trailing characters matching the given CharMatcher from each returned substring.
